title: Global Exception Handling
content:
# Spring Boot Interview Prep

## Q1: Explain how the Spring IoC container resolves circular dependencies between singleton beans using the 3-Level Cache.

### Standard Answer
When instantiating Bean A, Spring places an ObjectFactory for A in the third cache (`singletonFactories`). During property injection, Spring notices A requires Bean B. It pauses A's creation and attempts to instantiate B. B requires A, so Spring checks caches. It finds A's factory in the third cache, generates a partially initialized reference to A, moves it to the second cache (`earlySingletonObjects`), and injects it into B. B completes initialization and is placed in the first cache (`singletonObjects`), allowing A's creation to complete.


## Q2: How does the system handle concurrent throughput anomalies under Global Exception Handling?

### Standard Answer
The system uses dynamic resource pools and queue-backoff throttles to maintain throughput boundaries, avoiding thread starvation.
